title: Build rule-sync orchestrator (R28 framework)
description: NEW services/rule-sync.ts is the canonical orchestrator for rule freshness across MCP tool entries / startup / doctor. Exports `ensureRulesFresh(projectRoot, opts?)` and `reconcileRules(projectRoot)`. Internal: mtime detection vs agents.meta.json hash → if stale: incremental rebuild (changed files only) → write granular ledger events (rule_content_changed / rule_added / rule_removed) with 500ms per-file debounce → invalidate ContextCache → return RuleSyncReport with structured warnings (typed code + file:line + action_hint, feeds R24). Throws RuleError (from FabricError taxonomy) on validation failures (frontmatter parse fail, duplicate stable_id). NO consumer wiring in this task — that lands in TASK-021/022/023.
scope: Layer 1 foundation — sync orchestrator framework; downstream consumers wired in TASK-021/022/023/024

files.change: NEW: export ensureRulesFresh(projectRoot, opts?: { mode?: 'incremental' | 'full' }), reconcileRules(projectRoot); type RuleSyncReport = { status: 'fresh' | 'reconciled' | 'errors', events: LedgerEvent[], warnings: StructuredWarning[], reconciled_files?: string[] }; 500ms debounce per file (Map<file, lastSyncTs>); throws RuleError on validation failures
NEW: tests for fresh path / stale incremental path / full scan path / debounce dedup / RuleError on bad frontmatter / structured warnings shape

criteria: ensureRulesFresh and reconcileRules exported with documented contracts
500ms per-file debounce verified (3 hash-identical writes within 500ms = 1 ledger event)
Invalid frontmatter throws RuleError with actionHint pointing to fab doctor --fix
Hash-identical save produces NO ledger event (revision-hash based dedup)
Granular ledger events written: rule_content_changed { stable_id, path, prev_hash, new_hash, changed_fields[], source }
All new tests pass in same commit
